criteria.evidence: Not surfaced. The plan names InstructorProfileController and Views YAML, but does not identify displaySessionSchedule or any equivalent schedule-table builder.
Not surfaced. No mention of instructorScheduleTableMarkup or a row/table helper; the plan assumes the table is driven by Views config.
Not surfaced. The plan vaguely mentions templates/preprocess hooks, but does not name acme_alloc_entity_view, hook_entity_view, or entity_view attachment behavior.
Not surfaced. No mention of field_instructor_schedule_date, schedule release, release date gating, or any visibility/date constraint.
Not surfaced. No mention of getCoordinatorData, coordinator data, or a coordinator-derived source for schedule information.
Names `web/modules/custom/acme_alloc/src/Entity/SeatTime.php`, `SeatTime::preSave()`, and describes `SeatTime` entities.
Explicitly identifies `field_delta` as the participant position/delta and says it is read via `->get('field_delta')->value`.

Mentions `SeatTimeForm::save()` and `SessionSlot::manageSeatTimes()`, but does not surface the expected save/assignment path symbols such as `saveSeatTime`, `assignSeatTime`, or `SessionAllocationParticipantAssignAction`.

Describes an inferred `SessionSlot::getFirstAvailableSeatTimeDelta()` algorithm, but does not name or use the expected available-position computation context such as `getAvailableSeatTimeOptions` or `SeatTimeStorage`.
Correctly notes duration depends on `field_is_priority_shift` and says priority shifts use 30 minutes versus 15 minutes.
Names `AvailabilityCalendarUniqueConstraint.php`, property `AvailabilityCalendarUniqueConstraint::$message`, and describes the validation constraint preserving uniqueness enforcement.
Names `AvailabilityCalendarUniqueConstraintValidator.php` and method `AvailabilityCalendarUniqueConstraintValidator::validate()`.
Explicitly cites `$availability_calendar_storage->getByUserAndSessionPeriod($value->getOwner(), $value->getSessionPeriod())`.
Notes that URL link generation pointing to the existing conflicting availability calendar must continue to function.
Does not name AvailabilityCalendarFieldDefaultWidget, formElement, FieldWidget, or otherwise surface the widget layer; it only mentions controller/form/interface/validator.
Names AvailabilityCalendarFieldItemInterface.php constant DEPARTURE_TIME_ALLOWED_DAYS as the single authoritative source.
States current value is [6], Saturday, using ISO-8601 where 6 = Saturday, and change to [5] Friday.
Names AvailabilityCalendarFieldConstraintValidator::validate() and describes enforcement via checking membership in DEPARTURE_TIME_ALLOWED_DAYS.
Names `prepareRow(Row $row)` in `AcmePaymentTransaction.php` and describes the switch in that method handling per-gateway payload normalization.
Names class `AcmePaymentTransaction` in `/acme_migrate/src/Plugin/migrate/source/AcmePaymentTransaction.php` as the migration source.
Describes `case 'stripe'` as PHP-serialized payload using `unserialize(..., ['allowed_classes' => FALSE])`, extracting `paymentRef` and `payMethod`.
Describes `case 'paypal'` parsing a pipe/delimited-style string via `explode('. ')` and `explode(' : ')`, with key/value normalization and filtering.
Identifies `acceptance_date` and `paypal_clearing_date` conversion using `DrupalDateTime` to Unix timestamps, also setting remote date source properties.
Names `web/modules/custom/acme_migrate/src/EventSubscriber/PostMigrationSubscriber.php` and class `PostMigrationSubscriber`.
States event subscription is bound to `MigrateEvents::POST_IMPORT` for migration ID `acme_commerce1_product_variation_session`.
Names method `createProducts(MigrateImportEvent $event)`.
Explicitly maps variation 1 to T2, variation 2 to T3, and adds variation 3 to T1.
Describes linking variations via `'variations' => [$id]` and one variation per parent product.
Names `InstructorManager::displaySessionSchedule()` and describes it as preparing/wrapping schedule display.
Names `InstructorManager::instructorScheduleTableMarkup()` and describes it as building the session schedule table rows.
No mention of `acme_alloc_entity_view`, `hook_entity_view`, or any entity view attach hook.
No mention of `field_instructor_schedule_date`, schedule release, or release-date gating.
No mention of `getCoordinatorData` or coordinator data/source.
Names `web/modules/custom/acme_alloc/src/Entity/SeatTime.php`, `SeatTime::preSave()`, `SeatTime::postSave()`, and says SeatTime extends `RevisionableContentEntityBase`.
Explicitly identifies `field_delta` as the recorded position field and describes comparing old/new delta values.
Names `SeatTimeForm::save()` and `AssignParticipantToSessionSlotsForm` as paths that create/update SeatTime entities, plus programmatic saves.
Names `SeatTimeStorage::getAvailableSeatTimeOptions()` and describes delta gaps/capacity/first-open-position behavior.
States durations are `30 mins for priority shifts, 15 mins for regular`.
Mentions a duplicate availability uniqueness constraint and possible field/Symfony validation constraint mechanism, though without the exact constraint class name.
Mentions a constraint validator, Symfony validator pattern, and possible validate() method as the place generating the message.
Does not name or describe getByUserAndSessionPeriod or an equivalent specific lookup method.
Only says the conflicting existing availability record should continue to be identified/referenced; it does not surface a link-to-existing behavior or link symbol/API.
Names `AvailabilityCalendarFieldDefaultWidget::formElement()` and says the widget uses the constant to determine whether to show `departure_time`.
Names `AvailabilityCalendarFieldItemInterface::DEPARTURE_TIME_ALLOWED_DAYS` as the rule constant.
States current `DEPARTURE_TIME_ALLOWED_DAYS = [6]`, explains PHP `format('N')` where `6 = Saturday`, and proposes changing it to `[5]`.
Names `AvailabilityCalendarFieldConstraintValidator::validate()` and describes it enforcing the rule by checking the constant and requiring `departure_time`.
Names `AcmePaymentTransaction::prepareRow()` as the change point and describes adding a case in its switch.
Names source class/file: `web/modules/custom/acme_migrate/src/Plugin/migrate/source/AcmePaymentTransaction.php`.
Describes Stripe path as PHP-serialized payload using `unserialize`, extracting `paymentRef` and `payMethod`.
Describes Paypal path as parsing message text by `. ` and ` : ` into key-value pairs.
States `acceptance_date` and `paypal_clearing_date` are converted to Unix timestamps via `DrupalDateTime::getTimestamp()` with exception handling.
Names `web/modules/custom/acme_migrate/src/EventSubscriber/PostMigrationSubscriber.php` and `PostMigrationSubscriber::createProducts()`.
States `createProducts()` is subscribed to `MigrateEvents::POST_IMPORT` and fires only for the session variation migration.
Explicitly identifies method `PostMigrationSubscriber::createProducts()` as the change location.
Describes adding T1 by following existing conditional product creation pattern for T2 and T3 session-level products.
Describes loading `ProductVariation` entities and associating products via the `variations` array.
Names `web/modules/custom/acme_alloc/src/InstructorManager.php` and `InstructorManager::displaySessionSchedule()` as the method that builds the session schedule table.
Does not mention `instructorScheduleTableMarkup` or any concrete row/table helper; only generically discusses table rendering and possible templates.
Does not mention `acme_alloc_entity_view`, `hook_entity_view`, or `entity_view` attachment behavior.
Does not mention `field_instructor_schedule_date`, schedule release, or release-date gating.
Does not mention `getCoordinatorData` or coordinator-derived data.
Names `seat_time` entity, `SeatTimeInterface`, and `SeatTimeStorage.php`.
Explicitly states `field_delta` stores the position and is the single source of truth.
Names `AssignParticipantToSessionSlotsForm::saveSeatTime()` and `SessionAllocationParticipantAssignAction::saveSeatTime()` as the two save locations.
Names `SeatTimeStorage.php:getAvailableSeatTimeOptions()` and describes iterating from `0` to `max_capacity - 1` while excluding taken positions.
States start time uses `delta * session_duration` where duration is `15 min (normal) or 30 min (priority shift)`.
Names `AvailabilityCalendarUniqueConstraint.php`, property `$message`, and describes it as the validation constraint/secondary enforcement layer.
Names `AvailabilityCalendarUniqueConstraintValidator.php` and `AvailabilityCalendarUniqueConstraintValidator::validate()`.
Explicitly identifies and preserves `getByUserAndSessionPeriod($value->getOwner(), $value->getSessionPeriod())`.
Describes the existing calendar link, preserving the `:url` placeholder and route parameter for the pre-existing calendar.
Names `AvailabilityCalendarFieldDefaultWidget` and describes it as controlling conditional rendering of the `departure_time` field element based on the session date.
Names `DEPARTURE_TIME_ALLOWED_DAYS` in `AvailabilityCalendarFieldItemInterface.php` and proposes changing it from `[6]` to `[5]`.
Explicitly states `DEPARTURE_TIME_ALLOWED_DAYS = [6]` and that `6 = Saturday` in PHP `N` format.
Names `AvailabilityCalendarFieldConstraintValidator::validate()` and shows it reads `DEPARTURE_TIME_ALLOWED_DAYS` to enforce missing `departure_time` violations.
Names `AcmePaymentTransaction::prepareRow()` as the method to change and discusses normalization inside `prepareRow()`.
Names the source class/file: `web/modules/custom/acme_migrate/src/Plugin/migrate/source/AcmePaymentTransaction.php` and `AcmePaymentTransaction`.
Correctly describes Stripe path: serialized PHP object in `payload`, `unserialize(... allowed_classes => FALSE)`, extracts `paymentRef` and `payMethod`.
Correctly describes Paypal path: plain-text `message`, parsed by delimiters into key/value pairs, normalized into serialized `payload`.
Explicitly names `DrupalDateTime::getTimestamp()` and conversion of `acceptance_date` / `paypal_clearing_date` to Unix timestamps with NULL on parse failure.
Names `web/modules/custom/acme_migrate/src/EventSubscriber/PostMigrationSubscriber.php` and `PostMigrationSubscriber::createProducts()`.
States event subscription must remain on `MigrateEvents::POST_IMPORT` and migration ID check for `acme_commerce1_product_variation_session`.
Identifies method `PostMigrationSubscriber::createProducts()` as the code to change.
Describes existing T2 and T3 products and adds T1 with title convention `Acme-CERT ({LEVEL})`.
Describes products wrapping variations via the `variations` array and proposes `ProductVariation::load(3)` with `variations => [3]`.
